actionable_feedback: Open agent_eval_3.xlsx in an environment with a spreadsheet library (e.g., openpyxl) and ensure all intermediate calculations are implemented as spreadsheet formulas (not only cached values).

In the memo (expert_memo.md) add explicit sensitivity-analysis tables for +/-10% and +/-20% perturbations for each option with either the formulas shown inline or direct cell references to the spreadsheet so graders can reproduce results without opening the workbook.
Document the exact per-row 'residual_gap' formula and the aggregation/composite score formula in the memo, with row-level citations to the spreadsheet cells used for each intermediate value.

Ensure the spreadsheet exposes visible formulas (not just values) for key computations (post-option metrics, residual_gap, quality_penalty, aggregate/composite scores) and include a short verification table in the memo showing a sample row calculation cross-checked between memo text and spreadsheet cells.

If library availability is an environment constraint, include a small CSV or plain-text appendix that shows the computation steps and formulas for at least one representative row and for the sensitivity perturbations so graders can validate arithmetic without parsing the xlsx.

Semantic confidence reduced because the spreadsheet could not be parsed (openpyxl unavailable) and the memo preview lacks explicit +/-10% and +/-20% sensitivity tables and an explicit residual_gap formula. Addressing these will improve verifiability and semantic score.
